issues.title: Create Plone site administrator guide

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue aims to create a comprehensive guide for Plone site administrators. It will cover navigation through control panels and details on installing add-ons. The guide should include screenshots where possible, according to related implementation plans (PLIP).

## What needs to be done
- Draft the content for the Administrator guide, ensuring it clearly explains how to navigate control panels and install add-ons.
- Consider incorporating a section on creating custom control panels.
- Reconcile and integrate content with any relevant existing discussions or documents, particularly with issue #1768.

## Helpful background
Contributors should familiarize themselves with Plone's existing documentation and the ongoing discussions about the Admin Guide, especially in relation to merging topics with the Developer Guide. There may be some confusion in the requirements and direction, so clarity on the structure is essential.

## Good first steps
1. Review the existing contributing guidelines in the repository.
2. Explore the Plone site to get hands-on experience as an administrator.
3. Check out relevant issues and discussions for context, especially issues #3987, #1768, and #4131.
4. Start drafting initial sections of the guide and share your progress for feedback!
```

issues.title: z3c.form fields with a default value cannot be set to None or ""

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue addresses a bug where `z3c.form` fields with a default value can't be set to `None` or an empty string `("")`. When users attempt to clear a field and save it, the default value is incorrectly restored upon re-editing the form.

## What needs to be done
We need to investigate and fix the underlying issue in `z3c.form` that causes default values to be reapplied even when users want to clear fields. This is currently recognized as a bug affecting multiple scenarios in Plone related to custom content types.

## Helpful background
The problem has been noted in various forms, affecting both querying behavior and fields such as `TextLine`. Members of the community have managed to identify that the issue arises with certain versions of `z3c.form`—versions 3.2.11 and later currently exhibit this bug, while 3.2.9 does not. However, there may be additional complexities not yet fully understood.

## Good first steps
1. Review the existing comments and check out the discussed versions of `z3c.form` to understand which changes led to the bug.
2. Test the implementation with `z3c.form` version 3.2.9 to confirm it behaves as expected.
3. Collaborate with other contributors for insights on the code and potential fixes. 
4. Document any findings in your review process for clarity and future reference.

issues.title: PLIP: Keyword Manager

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on implementing a **Keyword Manager** for Plone in the Volto framework. The purpose is to create a tool for managing keywords, enhancing user experience when categorizing and searching content.

## What needs to be done
Develop the base functionality of the **Products.PloneKeywordManager**. Discussions have included possible integrations into bulk actions for folders. The implementation will be done first as an add-on to support a client's needs before moving it into the core application.

## Helpful background
A keyword manager helps users manage keywords efficiently, especially in environments with many keywords. Discussion around the UX and integration suggests there are varying perspectives on features, such as a "show similar keywords" option. Feedback and user experience insights are still being gathered.

## Good first steps
1. **Familiarize yourself** with Plone and the Volto framework.
2. Review the existing discussions and comments for important context.
3. Start by developing a prototype based on the low-fidelity UX design shared in the comments.
4. Coordinate with contributors already involved, like Timo Stollenwerk, to gain insights and establish collaboration.

issues.title: Plone 7 frontend (Seven)

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on modernizing the Plone frontend to create a new version called "Seven" that embraces current web development standards, specifically using React Router 7. The goal is to replace outdated libraries and establish a more efficient and flexible architecture for the Plone CMS.

## What needs to be done
The plan includes significant updates to the existing Volto framework, such as:
- Transitioning to a modern bundler, Vite.
- Creating a pluggable standalone app for the CMS user interface.
- Refactoring components and introducing a new theming system without Semantic UI.
- Enhancing the overall content editing experience and ensuring compatibility with existing sites.

## Helpful background
This proposal is part of the Plone Improvement Proposal (PLIP) format. The discussion around this issue has included various perspectives on the direction of the project. Some responses are still ongoing and involve clarifications regarding the "headless" nature of the future setup.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the existing **Volto** framework and its components.
2. Read through the [Seven PLIP discussion](https://github.com/plone/volto/issues/6638) to get insight into what's already been proposed.
3. Consider contributing by drafting documentation or exploring ways to implement compatibility with current add-ons.
4. Engage in the conversation by asking questions if you’re unclear about specific technical aspects.
```

issues.title: Refactor class components to functional components

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on refactoring class-based components in Volto to functional components. This change will facilitate the use of React hooks, allowing for improved functionality and easier future upgrades.

## What needs to be done
You will need to convert defined class components to functional components while maintaining their lifecycle and behavior. It is crucial to provide Cypress acceptance tests for any components that currently lack them. If any component contracts change (such as props), proper documentation is required.

## Helpful background
The transition from class-based to functional components in React allows for better management of state and side effects through hooks. This issue has seen significant work already, but some components remain to be refactored.

**Note:** The current discussion indicates that work on this issue is on hold until the next alpha release of Volto 19, with some PRs already submitted and others pending.

## Good first steps
1. Review the existing PRs related to this issue to understand current progress.
2. Familiarize yourself with the Plone and Volto contribution guidelines, especially around component structure.
3. Consider picking one of the unfactored components to refactor or assist in reviewing existing pull requests.
4. Engage in discussions if you have questions or need clarification on refactoring approaches.
```

issues.title: PLIP: Make Products.CMFEditions a Core Add-on

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue discusses a proposal to move **Products.CMFEditions** from being a core dependency of **Products.CMFPlone** to a core add-on. Currently, every Plone site has it active, which adds overhead even when versioning is not used. The proposal aims to allow sites that don't need versioning to exclude it without losing functionality.

## What needs to be done
To achieve this, several code changes are required in various packages. Important tasks include:
- Removing hard dependencies on **Products.CMFEditions** from **Products.CMFPlone**.
- Implementing conditional imports and fallback mechanisms to ensure functionality without errors if **CMFEditions** is absent.
- Updating tests to skip assertions that assume **CMFEditions** is installed.

## Helpful background
There's a concern about performance overhead due to **CMFEditions**, especially regarding object creation time. While there's some ambiguity in comments about the exact impact of versioning on all content types, tests and benchmarks hint at significant performance implications for sites using versioning.

## Good first steps
1. **Review the PLIP documentation** to understand the process for contributing changes to Plone.
2. Familiarize yourself with the **Products.CMFPlone** repository structure and where dependencies are declared.
3. Start by making small changes, such as modifying dependencies and implementing conditional import patterns.
4. Join the discussion to clarify any uncertainties and seek guidance from the community.
```

issues.title: Move new CSRF docs from Classic UI to Backend?

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue explores whether the newly created Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) documentation, currently under "Classic UI," should be moved to "Backend" and updated for Volto. The discussions indicate that CSRF protection may not apply to the frontend but needs clarification in the documentation.

## What needs to be done
1. **Move the CSRF documentation** from Classic UI to Backend.
2. **Update the content** to specify how CSRF is managed in the Volto environment and clarify its application in Plone 6's different frontends.

## Helpful background
Currently, the documentation doesn’t adequately reflect how CSRF protection works with the Plone REST API and various frontends. Ongoing discussions suggest that while CSRF checks are generally not necessary in JSON web services, the framework requires further attention to properly document this.

## Good first steps
1. Review and familiarize yourself with the current CSRF documentation [here](https://6.docs.plone.org/classic-ui/csrf.html).
2. Begin drafting proposed changes to the content based on discussions and insights from contributors.
3. Engage with the team for clarification on any points of confusion, particularly regarding the backend's CSRF protections.
```

issues.title: Create User Manual with screenshots and videos for Plone 6

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on creating a comprehensive User Manual for Plone 6, including screenshots and videos. Currently, much of the documentation is outdated, particularly related to the Classic UI. By using automated tools, we can streamline the process of keeping this manual accurate and accessible.

## What needs to be done
1. Investigate tools like Cypress and Playwright that can automate the capturing of screenshots and videos.
2. Port the existing Plone 5 user manual to Plone 6, ensuring it’s in MyST format with SEO headers.
3. Set up automation via GitHub workflows to update these visuals with every pull request.

## Helpful background
Older documentation can be daunting to update continuously. The goal here is to leverage automation to enhance the user experience without overloading users with visuals. Discussions suggest a balance between visuals and narrative text is essential, addressing concerns that too many visuals can clutter documentation.

## Good first steps
1. Explore the existing [Content Editing training guide](https://training.plone.org/content-editing/index.html) for reuse opportunities.
2. Familiarize yourself with Cypress Studio and other mentioned tools.
3. Join the conversation at PloneConf 2025 to discuss the direction and gather feedback.
4. Consider collaborating with others who are interested in this effort.

Feel free to ask questions if anything is unclear!
```

issues.title: Include diazo documentation in Classic backend documentation

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue seeks to include the Diazo documentation into the Plone documentation repository. Currently, the Diazo documentation is hosted separately at [diazo.org](https://docs.diazo.org/en/latest/), and there is a suggestion to integrate it more closely with Plone’s documentation.

## What needs to be done
1. Find and contact the maintainers of the Diazo documentation on ReadTheDocs to request maintainer access.
2. Convert the existing Diazo documentation to MyST format, which is a format compatible with markdown.
3. Decide where to add these documents in the Plone repository as a Git submodule.
4. Set up the submodule in the `plone/documentation` following [these guidelines](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/documentation/admins.html#importing-external-docs-with-submodules).

## Helpful background
Diazo is a tool for theming in Plone, and integrating its documentation could enhance the user experience. The discussions are ongoing, and there might be additional criteria that need clarification.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the Diazo documentation at [diazo.org](https://docs.diazo.org/en/latest/).
2. Check the [Plone documentation contribution guide](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/documentation/) for setup instructions.
3. Consider reaching out to the current maintainers for guidance as you begin this work.

Feel free to ask questions if you're unsure about anything!
```

issues.title: Search the control panel to find a setting

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue proposes adding a search feature in the Plone control panel to allow users to locate specific settings quickly, similar to the search function in macOS's System Settings. The idea is still in the discussion phase, and there are various viewpoints on how to implement it effectively.

## What needs to be done
We need to design a functionality that lets users search for specific settings and see which control panels contain them. There is currently no clear implementation path, and discussions suggest starting with a simple search bar that filters existing settings, eventually moving towards a more advanced solution.

## Helpful background
Contributors have mentioned the importance of both global and filtered search options within the control panel. There is no existing widget that mimics the desired functionality, but notable methods include indexing fields from control panel forms.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the discussions in the issue. 
2. Check the [Contributing Guide for Plone](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/index.html) and the [Volto contributions](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/volto.html) to understand the contribution process.
3. Consider trying to implement a basic search feature that filters through current settings; additional complexity can be added later.
4. Engage with existing contributors for feedback and guidance throughout the development process.
```

issues.title: Automate screenshots and videos

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on enhancing Plone documentation by automating the capture of current screenshots and videos of the user interface for both Classic UI and Volto. The goal is to ensure that whenever there’s a change in the UI, contributors are notified in pull requests or during testing.

## What needs to be done
We need to develop a system that automatically captures screenshots of the Plone interface and detects any changes. Suggestions have included using tools like Cypress and Playwright. A recent tool, Heroshot, has also gained attention for its potential integration with Sphinx documentation.

## Helpful background
Historically, this feature existed in Plone 5 but is now unsupported. There's ongoing exploration of automating this process using modern tools. The discussion includes various contributors testing and proposing ideas, making this a collaborative effort.

## Good first steps
1. **Familiarize yourself** with the tools mentioned, especially Cypress, Playwright, and Heroshot.
2. **Test Heroshot** with Plone documentation by creating a new branch and running initial tests, as suggested by @stevepiercy.
3. **Document your findings** on Heroshot’s usability and report any bugs or enhancement suggestions.
4. Start engaging in discussions on the existing PRs or issues to get acclimated with the project.
```

issues.title: Move videos from the User Manual to a CDN or YouTube

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue discusses moving videos from the Plone user manual to an external hosting solution, like a Content Delivery Network (CDN) or YouTube. This is to reduce the repository's size, which currently contains videos that contribute significant disk usage.

## What needs to be done
1. **Audit** existing video assets in the documentation.
2. **Select** a hosting option (CDN or YouTube).
3. **Update** all references to the videos in the documentation.
4. **Document** the recommended approach for future video uploads.

## Helpful background
The current video storage contributes about 8MB to the repository size, which can hinder development. This issue is marked as a complex PLIP (Product Life-cycle Improvement Proposal) task, meaning it requires careful coordination among various teams within Plone, including Documentation, Admin, and Marketing.

## Good first steps
1. **Familiarize yourself** with Plone and its documentation systems.
2. **Join discussions** with other contributors to understand hosting preferences or constraints.
3. **Explore the GitHub link** on removing large files from history to grasp the complexities involved.
4. **Be prepared** for potential coordination efforts if you plan to contribute.

issues.title: Unpin sphinx in requirements.txt

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue discusses the need to unpin the Sphinx dependency from `sphinx<9` in the `requirements.txt` file. The current pinning is a temporary solution to address an underlying issue that needs resolution. The ultimate goal is to finalize and release Plone Sphinx Theme v2.0.0, removing the dependency on the Sphinx Book Theme.

## What needs to be done
The primary task is to remove the version restriction for Sphinx in the `requirements.txt` file once the related work on Plone Sphinx Theme v2.0.0 is complete. However, it's important to note that the specifics of what remains to be done and any contributing guidelines are currently unclear.

## Helpful background
Sphinx is a documentation generator, and pinning it helps avoid potential compatibility issues. The Sphinx Book Theme is an additional theme that complicates the current setup, making it essential to address it to move forward.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with Sphinx and the Plone Sphinx Theme.
2. Check for current development branches or documentation related to this issue.
3. Engage with existing discussions for context, but be prepared that the requirements may shift as this is a complex area with an ambiguous roadmap. 

Feel free to reach out if you have questions or need guidance!

issues.title: View for Listing block

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on implementing the view for the Listing block in the Plone Volto project. It is designated as part of a more complex future project known as "Seven."

## What needs to be done
The specific tasks for this issue are currently unclear and may involve a particular variation of the Listing block. There are indications that the author, [ebrehault](https://github.com/ebrehault), is already working on it, so coordination with them is essential to avoid duplicated effort.

## Helpful background
Due to the complexity labeled in this issue, it may not be suitable for first-time contributors. Discussions have pointed out that there are relevant existing issues (e.g., #4478 on listing/teaser styles), but details about these connections remain vague. Familiarity with the overall project and its guidelines is recommended.

## Good first steps
If you're interested in contributing:
1. Review the [First-time contributors](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/first-time.html) guidelines.
2. Familiarize yourself with [Contributing to Plone](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/index.html) and [Contributing to Volto](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/volto.html).
3. Consider reaching out to [ebrehault](https://github.com/ebrehault) for insights before proceeding, as clear tasks for this issue may need further clarification.

issues.title: [Seven] - Make APISUFFIX configurable via environment variables

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on making the API suffix in Volto easily configurable via environment variables. Currently, the default suffix (`/++api++`) is hardcoded, which complicates integration with alternative backends like [Nick](https://nickcms.org) or [Guillotina](https://plone.org/why-plone/plone-ecosystem/guillotina).

## What needs to be done
The goal is to implement a way for developers to set the API suffix through an environment variable (e.g., `PLONE_API_SUFFIX`). This would allow for easier customization and flexibility when using different Plone RestAPI-compatible backends.

## Helpful background
- Now, to use backends that don’t rely on the `++api++` prefix, a workaround is needed, such as defining a custom `PloneClient`. 
- There is an existing variable, `RAZZLE_LEGACY_TRAVERSE`, for similar purposes in Volto but it’s unclear if it fully meets the needs for the `@plone/client`.

## Good first steps
1. Review the current implementation in `@plone/client` to understand how it handles API calls.
2. Explore how `RAZZLE_LEGACY_TRAVERSE` functions as a reference.
3. Create a prototype for setting the API suffix through an environment variable.
4. Make sure to check the [First-Time Contributors Guide](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/first-time.html) for guidance on your contributions.
5. Consider documenting your changes and coordinate with other contributors as needed.
```

issues.title: Implement non-schema-driven control panels

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on implementing non-schema-driven control panels in the Plone Volto project. Control panels are settings interfaces that allow users to manage configuration options without being tied to specific data models (schemas). This could enhance flexibility and user customization.

## What needs to be done
You will need to design and implement these control panels to manage different aspects, such as add-ons, users, and groups. Since the issue is open and there is limited detail and no specific guidance in the comments, you might encounter some ambiguity in requirements.

## Helpful background
Understanding control panels in Plone is important for this task. They allow administrators to adjust settings easily. Non-schema-driven panels can provide a more fluid user experience by enabling custom configurations without strict data structures.

## Good first steps
1. **Familiarize yourself with existing control panels** in the Volto codebase to see how they are currently structured.
2. **Review the existing documentation** on Plone and Volto to better understand implementation context.
3. **Reach out to the author, [ksuess](https://github.com/ksuess)**, or the community for clarification on any uncertainties.
4. **Consider drafting a proposal** for your approach before starting implementation, as this could help clarify expectations.

issues.title: Metadata block or easy way to insert document metadata inside text block

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on enhancing Volto by introducing a way to insert document metadata into text blocks. The goal is to allow users to easily display metadata, such as publication dates or tags, within content areas. This could greatly improve how dynamic pages are built.

## What needs to be done
The enhancement involves creating a new block type or adding a button in the text block that pulls content from metadata fields. Discussions indicate that integrating this feature into the core would be beneficial, although there are some reservations about adding extra blocks.

## Helpful background
Current discussions mention existing add-ons like [volto-metadata-block](https://github.com/eea/volto-metadata-block) and [volto-slate-metadata-mentions](https://github.com/eea/volto-slate-metadata-mentions), which provide similar features. However, there is a strong sentiment that having metadata functionalities in core would simplify the user experience.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the existing add-ons mentioned for additional context.
2. Review the current implementation of text blocks in Volto.
3. Discuss with other contributors about how best to integrate this feature into the core.
4. Consider starting by drafting a proposal or mockups for the new block/button functionality.
```

issues.title: Add details about each of the default behaviors

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue involves enhancing the Plone documentation by adding detailed explanations of the default behaviors within the system. Current descriptions may be too brief, and there's discussion about possibly separating these details from the general overview of how to use behaviors.

## What needs to be done
You will need to write comprehensive descriptions for each of the default behaviors in Plone, explaining what they do and their significance. It's important to ensure these explanations are clear and accessible, especially for those new to Plone.

## Helpful background
Behaviors in Plone are a way to extend the functionality of content types. This issue is aimed at improving understanding of these features. However, the discussion indicates that a deep understanding of these behaviors is required, as it can be a complex topic.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with Plone's current documentation on behaviors.
2. Read more about contributing to Plone by following [First-time contributors](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/first-time.html).
3. Reach out for feedback or clarification on any points that seem ambiguous or unclear.
4. Begin drafting your detailed descriptions for the behaviors once you feel comfortable with the material.
```

issues.title: Create redirects of `docs.plone.org` to `6.docs.plone.org`

contributor_summary.summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on creating 301 redirects from `docs.plone.org` to `6.docs.plone.org` to help guide users and search engines to the updated documentation. It aims to deal with existing links that might still reference the old documentation.

## What needs to be done
We need to set up 301 redirects for various URLs to ensure users are sent to the correct pages on `6.docs.plone.org`. There is also a need to track any resulting 404 errors so we can refine our redirects based on real user data.

## Helpful background
Redirects are essential for maintaining user experience and SEO when content is moved. A 301 redirect signifies that a page has permanently moved to a new location. Current discussions highlight the importance of tracking which URLs generate 404 errors after the redirects are implemented.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the current redirects and the URLs needed.
2. Explore existing discussions for insights on the tracking strategy using Matomo.
3. Consider testing how to generate 404 errors for URLs that aren't redirected, to gather tracking data effectively.
4. Collaborate with the assigned contributors, such as @fredvd and @polyester, for guidance on implementation.
```
